qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Debug API database methods lack authorization enabling unauthorized data access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated debug RPC database reads in internal/ethapi/dbapi.go, while the advisory patch concerns p2p blob transaction handling for invalid KZG proofs in core/txpool validation and eth/fetcher transaction cleanup.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: RLP decoder lacks size limits enabling DoS via unbounded memory allocation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets generic RLP Decode size-limit behavior and allocation in rlp/decode.go, not the advisory's invalid blob transaction KZG proof validation and peer-disconnect root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncontrolled scrypt KDF parameters allow DoS via resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns attacker-controlled keystore scrypt parameters in accounts/keystore/passphrase.go, while the advisory patch concerns malicious p2p blob transaction messages with invalid KZG proofs.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory's fixed commit adds ErrKZGVerificationError handling for invalid blob sidecar KZG proofs, stops processing the offending transaction batch, and disconnects the violating p2p peer. The qualifying findings cover debug RPC database authorization, generic RLP decoder allocation behavior, and keystore scrypt parameter resource exhaustion instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mr7q-c9w9-wh4h describes a malicious p2p message that can crash or shut down a vulnerable go-ethereum node.

Referenced commit abeb78c647e354ed922726a1d719ac7bc64a07e2 includes the security-relevant change 'core/txpool: drop peers on invalid KZG proofs', modifying core/txpool/validation.go and eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go.
Session search found no qualifying report evidence for KZG, blob transaction sidecars, txpool validation, tx_fetcher delivery cleanup, or peer disconnection on invalid proof errors.
debug_db_unauthorized_access, rlp_decode_dos, and scrypt_kdf_dos describe different vulnerable files and root-cause mechanisms.
